topic_title: How LLMs work — tokens, training, and inference

citation_density.detail: All three resources ([1] thenewstack.io, [2] medium.com, [3] bentoml.com) are cited. Primary [1] cited multiple times in §5.1. §5.1 through §5.5, §6, and §7 all carry citation markers. Coverage well above 60%. No fabricated citations detected.

soft_findings.resolution: MITIGATED — parenthetical plain-English definition added: '(one complete run of the input through all model layers to produce an output)'. Definition is adequate for beginner audience. Soft flag cleared.
Inline paraphrase 'a distribution of possible next tokens' remains adequate. Not a specialist AI concept requiring prior sequence introduction. Acceptable.
One-liner with parenthetical definition: 'batching (processing multiple users' decode steps together in parallel)'. Named-and-deferred form. Acceptable.

length_sanity.detail: Estimated 2270 words. Within the 1800–3500 word range specified for standard profile corpus. No change from attempt 1 (only two sentences modified).

topic_scope.detail: Temperature fix verified: §5.3 line 80 now reads 'the degree of randomness is controlled by temperature (covered in topic 3.6).' The word 'temperature' is not bolded, and there is no defining clause or appositive — only a bare name plus a topic pointer. This satisfies the mention-and-defer rule. No other scope_creep candidates detected. 'Knowledge cutoff' (§5.5) is a one-line observable consequence of training, not taught as a standalone concept. 'Batching' and 'system prompt' are single passing mentions with plain-language context. All clear.

beginner_readability.detail: The attempt-1 soft finding ('forward pass' undefined) is resolved by the parenthetical definition added in §7. All 14 new concepts introduced by this topic are defined at first use with plain-language explanations before technical phrasing. No jargon dumps detected (no paragraph stacks 3+ new terms). No academic hedging phrases detected. Sentence length is consistently short and direct.

verdict: PASS — all five checks clear on attempt 2. Hard fail on topic_scope from attempt 1 is resolved: 'temperature' is now bare and unbolded with a topic-3.6 pointer only.
